questionHtml:
Which is correct for the reaction H2O (g) → H2O (l) ?

 

A. Enthalpy increases and entropy increases.

B. Enthalpy decreases and entropy increases.

C. Enthalpy increases and entropy decreases.

D. Enthalpy decreases and entropy decreases.

meta.title: Which is correct for the reaction H2O (g) → H2O (l) ? A. Enthalpy increases and entropy increases. B. Enthalpy decreases and entropy increases. C. Enthalpy increases and entropy de
breadcrumbLabels: Reactivity 1.4.2—Change in Gibbs energy, ΔG, relates the energy that can be obtained from a chemical reaction to the change in enthalpy, ΔH, change in entropy, ΔS, and absolute temperature, T. Apply the equation ΔG⦵ = ΔH⦵ − TΔS⦵ to calculate unknown values of these terms.
